result:
Build completed successfully — `wpa_supplicant`, `wpa_cli`, and `wpa_passphrase` all linked. The pre-existing `wpa_cli.c:4087` logical-not warning is unrelated to the five compile errors (it's a pre-existing style warning, not a build failure) and doesn't affect the build being green.

All five compile errors fixed, each a genuine typo/omission (root cause, not a workaround):

1. `src/utils/eloop.c:615` — `timeout->time.seconds` → `.sec` (struct `os_reltime` has no `seconds` field; line 616 already used `.sec` correctly).
2. `wpa_supplicant/config_file.c:29` — `os_strlength(buf)` → `os_strlen(buf)` (undeclared function).
3. `src/rsn_supp/wpa.c:103` — `sm->pairwise_ciph` → `sm->pairwise_cipher` (no such struct member).
4. `wpa_supplicant/events.c:132` — `wpa_s->cur_ssid` → `wpa_s->current_ssid` (no such struct member; only usage of the wrong name in the file).
5. `wpa_supplicant/scan.c:1137` — `eloop_register_timeout(sec, usec, wpa_supplicant_scan, wpa_s)` was missing the required `user_data` argument; added trailing `NULL`, matching the pattern used at the other two call sites in the same file.

`make` in `wpa_supplicant/` now completes with no errors.
